Built for s…

I think we are past the point where “only people in San Francisco get AI” is true. 

AI users are in every industry & they have access to the same models. SF is far from the epicenter of many of the craziest use cases I have seen in science, law, finance, marketing, education…

I was talking to a room of senior accountants a couple months ago and 10% had OpenClaw installations.

Of course there are far more non-users and firms lag behind their people, but there is a sort of Silicon Valley assumption that no one else gets tech that doesn’t apply to AI.
The replies to the first post are insufferable slop of the highest order. Attention vampires feeding on our willingness to assume a comment means someone has something to say.
